text.title: Behavior Parity Verification Procedure
text.summary: Six-step procedure walking from "we want to rewrite this path" to "legacy removed". Each step has input artefacts, action, output artefact, and a decision-gate that blocks progression on unmet criteria.

step.name: define-observables
step.input: scope (endpoint or function), legacy implementation source.
step.action: Enumerate fields that count as behavior. List them in `templates/parity-report.md` under "Observable fields". Mark any field that is incidental (request_id, computed_at_ms) as ignored.
step.output: parity-report.md draft with observable_fields populated; ignored-list documented.
step.decision-gate: If observable_fields is empty OR ignored-list is missing — STOP. The diff layer cannot start.

step.name: write-normalizer
step.action: Implement `normalize(observable) → canonical` in code (Python/TS/Go). Rules: quantize timestamps to second precision, canonicalize UUIDs, sort unordered lists, apply business-meaningful tolerance to floats. Unit-test each rule. Reject any rule that rewrites to a constant.
step.output: normalizer.{py,ts,go} merged with unit tests covering each canonicalization rule.
step.decision-gate: If any rule rewrites a field to a constant OR tolerance > 0.1% of value without written justification — STOP and revise.

step.name: deploy-diff-sampler
step.action: Deploy the shadow router behind a feature flag at 0% traffic. Run a smoke test: synthetic request → legacy + new → normalize → diff → write to parity_diffs (or no-op if zero diff). Verify PII redaction in the sampler is in place BEFORE flipping flag > 0%.
step.output: parity_diffs table populated by smoke runs; redaction verified.
step.decision-gate: If any sampled row contains unredacted PII — STOP, wipe store, fix sampler.

step.name: staged-ramp
step.input: diff sampler live, flag plumbing ready.
step.action: Ramp 1% → 10% → 50% → 100%. At each stage, run for ≥24h prod-traffic AND ≥1000 compared requests. At end-of-stage: run cluster analysis, produce a stage parity-report, evaluate gate (diff_rate < 0.5% AND all clusters classified). Promote only if gate is green; freeze if not; revert if regression.
step.output: One parity-report.json per stage; cluster table per stage.
step.decision-gate: If diff_rate ≥ 0.5% OR any cluster is "open" — do NOT promote. Either fix the root cause or revert.

step.name: cluster-analysis
step.input: parity_diffs rows for the current stage.
step.action: Group rows by normalized-diff hash (or equivalent shape key). Top-10 clusters by sample_count. For each cluster: identify root cause OR write a justification why the divergence is acceptable. No silent acceptance.
step.output: clusters[] array populated in stage parity-report.
step.decision-gate: If any cluster lacks both a root-cause-fix AND a written justification — STOP.

step.name: sign-off-and-cutover
step.action: Peer or self review confirms: total_compared meets threshold, diff_rate within gate, all clusters resolved, error rate at parity. Record signed_off_by + signed_off_at. Schedule legacy removal in a subsequent release (not the same release as 100% cutover).
step.decision-gate: If sign-off is missing OR legacy-removal is scheduled for the same release — STOP. Decouple cutover and removal.

text.reference: Report skeleton authors fill per stage.
Starting point for the normalizer code.
